title: Waves, Math, Scramble, Snap & Select
description: Scroll-driven sine-wave text, CSS sibling-index() layouts, character-scramble type, elastic drag-snap physics, and a natively styleable <select>.

drops.title: Sine Corridor
drops.description: A scroll-tied phase equation drives two text columns into opposing sine waves, spotlighting the row nearest the centerline.
drops.concept: Reimplements the scroll-driven dual-wave text technique: each row's horizontal offset is sin(waveNumber*index + waveSpeed*scrollProgress*2π), with the two columns mirrored so they swing in opposite directions as the page scrolls. A glowing centerline highlights whichever row is currently closest to the viewport's vertical middle.
drops.source: tympanus.net/codrops — 'Building a Scroll-Driven Dual-Wave Text Animation with GSAP'

interaction.mouse: scroll the page to sweep the wave phase
interaction.touch: swipe/scroll on mobile drives the same wave motion

drops.title: Sibling Math
drops.description: Three layouts — staggered cascade, self-dividing tabs, radial menu — computed live by CSS sibling-index() and sibling-count().
drops.concept: Demonstrates the new CSS sibling-index()/sibling-count() functions, which expose an element's position and total sibling count directly to calc() and trig functions. A slider changes how many items are in each demo, and every layout — cascade delay, equal tab widths, and radial angle spacing — recalculates with zero JavaScript coordinate math on supporting browsers, with a JS fallback for others.
drops.source: Smashing Magazine — 'Advanced Tree Counting: Mathematical Layouts With sibling-index() And sibling-count()'

interaction.mouse: drag the item-count slider to add/remove siblings live
interaction.click: replay button restarts the cascade animation

drops.title: Scramble Set
drops.description: Hover any line and each character cycles through random glyphs before resolving letter by letter into the real word.
drops.concept: Recreates a character-scramble hover effect: on hover, every character independently cycles through a pool of random symbols for a configurable number of iterations before locking to its true value, with an adjustable reveal order (random, start, center, end) and character set.

interaction.mouse: hover any headline to trigger the scramble-to-reveal
interaction.click: no click needed — hover is the trigger
interaction.touch: tap a line on touch devices to trigger the effect

drops.title: Snap & Settle
drops.description: Drag and fling a chip — it coasts on inertia, decelerates, then springs into the nearest grid point with elastic overshoot.
drops.concept: Reimplements the spirit of drag-snap-with-inertia interactions: pointer velocity at release determines a coasting phase, after which a lightweight spring integrator (stiffness + damping) pulls the chip into the closest point on a tunable grid, producing a satisfying elastic settle rather than an abrupt snap.

interaction.mouse: drag a chip and release with velocity to fling it
interaction.click: click-drag is the only interaction — no separate click action
interaction.touch: full touch drag support via Pointer Events

drops.title: Native Picker
drops.description: Three real <select> elements restyled from the inside with appearance:base-select — status dots, avatars and color swatches.
drops.concept: Uses the newly shipped appearance:base-select CSS value to expose a native <select>'s internals — the closed button, the ::picker(select) popover, and each <option> — as fully styleable parts, including a rotating ::picker-icon and custom ::checkmark. Browsers without support silently fall back to the default select, which is the built-in progressive enhancement.
drops.source: Chrome for Developers — 'The <select> element can now be customized with CSS'

interaction.mouse: click any select to open its custom-styled popover
interaction.click: click an option to select it — native keyboard nav still works
interaction.touch: tap to open, tap an option to select on mobile
